statement: | CHKP | CRC32-C (self-descriptor) | Checkpoint self-integrity; this is the Merkle root |
correct: CHKP self-descriptor algorithm is 8-byte CRC64, not CRC32-C. finding #332; structure_reference.md line 151

statement: [SHA-256] Available as a format-time option from the Insider build onward (VBR 0x2A = 0x04).
correct: SHA-256 is a format-time option on regular Win11 24H2 v3.14 (non-Insider). image_feature_matrix.csv: win11refs2g_sha256checksums & win11refs2t64ksha256checksums are v3.14, is_sha256=True, is_insider=F

statement: Because it has no parent to store its checksum, it uses a CRC32-C self-descriptor instead of the CRC64 used for all child pages.
correct: CHKP self-descriptor is an 8-byte CRC64 (distinct from the per-page page-reference CRC64), not CRC32-C. finding #332 / structure_reference L151.

statement: CRC32-C (Castagnoli) Used exclusively for self-descriptors in the checkpoint (CHKP) and superblock (SUPB).
correct: SUPB/CHKP self-descriptors use an 8-byte CRC64, not CRC32-C. CRC32-C is only the optional per-block integrity-stream data checksum. finding #332.

statement: SHA-256 ... Available as a format-time option from the Insider build onward (VBR 0x2A = 0x04).
correct: SHA-256 is available on v3.14 (Win11 24H2), not Insider-only: two v3.14 corpus images formatted with SHA-256 (win11refs2g_sha256checksums, win11refs2t64ksha256checksums, both is_insider=False). image_

statement: it uses a CRC32-C self-descriptor instead of the CRC64 used for all child pages
correct: The checkpoint self-descriptor is ALSO an 8-byte CRC64 (distinct from per-page page-ref CRC64), not CRC32-C. finding #332 (E2).

statement: SHA-256: Available as a format-time option from the Insider build onward (VBR 0x2A = 0x04).
correct: SHA-256 is a v3.14/24H2 format option, not Insider-only: image_feature_matrix has non-Insider v3.14 SHA-256 images (win11refs2g_sha256checksums); errata 'NOT Insider-specific' (errata l.376).

correct: The doc itself lists FIVE numbered methods (Method 1-5); Method 5 = B+-tree Node Slack Recovery (finding #334). The intro count 'four' is stale and contradicts the body.
